---
title: "QC_WES"
author: "Cenna Doornbos"
output: html_document
params:
  cnv_wes_ed_data_tab: Z:/data/iPSC_data/iPSC_CNV-WES/Data_integration/cnv_ed.csv
  metadata: ~/ipsc-data-analysis/20240514_Xomics iPSC Neurons_new_READ-ONLY.xlsx
---

## Additional QC analysis for WES data

#### Script parameters
```{r, params}
print(Sys.Date())
print(params)
```
### Libraries
```{r, read data}
library(readxl)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggrepel)
library(plotly)
```

### read data
```{r, read data}
data_frame_ed <- read.csv(params$cnv_wes_ed_data_tab)

metadata_wes <- read_excel(params$metadata, sheet = "Assay - genomics") %>%
  left_join(read_excel(params$metadata, sheet = "Sample - Induced pluripoten "), by = "sample identifier", suffix = c("_gen", "_sam")) %>%
  left_join(read_excel(params$metadata, sheet = "ObservationUnit"), by = "observation unit identifier", suffix = c("", "_OU")) %>%
  mutate("sample_name" = gsub("_Genomics", "", `assay identifier`), .after = "assay identifier")
```


```{r, sex match}
# adapt data to generate figure
df <- data_frame_ed %>%
  filter(chromosome == "X" | chromosome == "Y") %>%
  group_by(sample_name, chromosome) %>%
  summarise("n" = n(), .groups = "keep") %>%
  pivot_wider(id_cols = sample_name, names_from = chromosome, values_from = n, values_fill = 0) %>%
  mutate("measured sex" = if_else(Y > 1, "male", "female")) %>%
  left_join(metadata_wes, by = "sample_name") %>%
  dplyr::rename("sex" = gender) %>%
  mutate("label" = ifelse(`measured sex` != sex, sample_name, ""))
  

#generate sex match figure
p <- ggplot(df, aes(X, Y, colour = sex, label = label, text = sample_name, shape = `measured sex`)) +
  # geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.4, stroke = NA) +
  geom_jitter(size = 4, alpha = 0.4, stroke = NA) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 2) +
  labs(x = "X chromosome", y = "Y chromosome", title = "WES determined sex") +
  theme_minimal()

#save figure with labels
ggsave("Z:/data/iPSC_data/Results/QC/WES determined sex.pdf",
       plot = p + geom_text_repel(), width = 7, height = 7)

#display figure
ggplotly(p, tooltip = c("text", "colour", "shape"))
```
